summary: Comprehensive backend testing of Rintaki Anime Club API: 43/43 pytest tests passed covering health, auth (register/login/me/logout/google), auth dependency on protected endpoints (10 parametrised routes), rintaki feed proxy, forums (thread CRUD + replies + likes + notifications), points (me/daily-claim/leaderboard), events/newsletters/videos (seeded lists + admin-only guards + admin creates), messages (send/conversations/with + self/unknown errors), notifications read-all, profile update, members list, and admin stats. All endpoints return correct status codes, cookies are set properly (access_token + refresh_token httpOnly), points awarded correctly (welcome +10, thread +5, reply +2, daily +5), admin-only guards return 403 for members, unauthenticated returns 401. Emails lowercased server-side. No critical or minor issues found.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~715 lines; consider splitting into modules (auth, forums, points, events, newsletters, videos, messages, notifications, admin) once more features land (>700 LOC threshold).
Brute-force lockout uses IP+email key with a 5-attempt/15-minute window but is not reset on success for a partial window (acceptable; reset happens on success, confirmed).
`add_points` has no guard for negative balances; acceptable as all call sites use positive amounts, but worth documenting.

`POST /api/events` and `POST /api/newsletters` broadcast a notification per user inside a synchronous loop. For large user bases this will be slow; consider $insertMany with a generator or a background task.
`EventCreate.starts_at` accepts raw string without ISO validation; consider `datetime` type with Pydantic validator.

`/api/auth/google/session` trusts `data['email']`/`session_token` from provider response without key-presence checks; a malformed 200 response would raise KeyError -> 500. Wrap with explicit validation.
Cookies use `secure=True, samesite='none'` which is correct for the preview URL (HTTPS); local dev over HTTP would not receive cookies, but that's out of scope for this env.
test_credentials: Admin admin@rintaki.org / Admin@Rintaki2026 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md) - verified working. Test members created dynamically with TEST_ prefix.
seed_data_creation: Test members registered with TEST_ email prefix; a TEST_ thread, TEST_admin_event, TEST_nl newsletter and TEST_video were created against the live preview DB. These are harmless sample records; main agent may run a TEST_ prefix cleanup if desired.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 43 backend pytest cases pass against https://community-connect-257.preview.emergentagent.com. Reusable test file at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py. If emails are changed to preserve original case, adjust assertions (currently expect server lowercase). Seed data persists between runs; daily_claim test will fail for the admin if re-run same day against the same user (uses member_session_2 which is fresh each run, so unaffected).
